name: field
title: Field
description: Accessible form-field composition that gives one Input or Select a persistent label, optional guidance, shared error presentation, stable IDs, and observable interaction state.

intent.summary: Accessible form-field composition that gives one Input or Select a persistent label, optional guidance, shared error presentation, stable IDs, and observable interaction state.
useWhen: An Input, Select, or SelectMulti needs a persistent visible label in a form.
Help text or an error message must be programmatically associated with one control.
A form layout needs one consistent label-control-message rhythm.
avoidWhen: The component already owns its visible label and complete selection semantics, such as Checkbox, Radio, or Switch.
Several controls belong to one question; use a semantic fieldset and legend instead.
Text describes a region or group rather than one form control.

alternatives.when: Several related controls answer one question.
alternatives.reason: Use native fieldset and legend semantics, then compose Text only for supporting content.

accessibility: Slot exactly one form control. Field assigns its stable control ID and connects the visible label automatically.
Description and rendered error IDs are merged with any authored aria-describedby references rather than replacing them.
The error prop forwards aria-invalid and supported design-system control error styling; put visible error text on Field, not again on the child control.
Clicking the label focuses the slotted design-system control without opening a Select popup.
Do not use placeholder text as the only label. Keep label text concise and put format or consequence guidance in description.
states: Field exposes data-focused, data-filled, data-dirty, data-touched, data-invalid, data-disabled, and data-required hooks while the slotted control remains the source of value and interaction behavior.
Description remains visible during error so guidance and validation do not replace each other.
Field owns validation presentation but not validation timing; the form decides when to set error and errorMessage.
The label uses primary body-small emphasis, description uses secondary body-small text, and error uses negative body-small text.
responsiveBehavior: Field fills the width supplied by its parent and makes the slotted control fill that field width.
The form layout owns column count, wrapping, and responsive placement; Field keeps the vertical label-control-message composition intact.

customElements: Set fieldId only when another element must reference a predictable control ID; otherwise use the generated association.
Assign child component values and option arrays through their normal JavaScript properties.
react: Compose one DsInput, DsSelect, or DsSelectMulti child and control validation timing in the form owner.
angular: Compose the generated Field adapter around the control adapter and its matching Angular value accessor.

description.description: Optional guidance associated with the slotted control.

error.description: Invalid visual and accessible state forwarded to supported ds controls.

errorMessage.description: Visible error associated with the slotted control while error is true.

fieldId.description: Explicit ID for the slotted control; generated when omitted.

label.description: Persistent visible label for the single slotted control.

peerDependencies.frameworks: Custom Elements; React 18/19 wrappers; Angular 19-22 standalone adapters.
